page: Vultr vhf-1c-1gb is a virtual machine within the High Frequency Compute family, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 1 shared Intel Core Processor (Skylake, IBRS) physical core operating at 2.0 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The system includes 1.0 GB of system memory and 32 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU hardware is included. The CPU features AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets, but lacks AVX-512 extensions.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the single-core stress-ng CPU performance sits in the average tier with a score of 1865.79. However, multi-core performance is limited to the same score due to the single-core configuration, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also in the bottom 10%, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 132.54 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 13.63 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores are similarly positioned in the bottom 10%, reflecting the entry-level nature of this shared-resource configuration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a low-cost entry point for basic computing tasks, trading off high-throughput performance for budget-friendly resource allocation. The shared CPU allocation and 1.0 GB memory limit its capability under sustained or multi-threaded workloads, as demonstrated by the low database operations and static web serving throughput benchmarks. This server is suitable for lightweight applications, basic web serving, development testing, and small utility scripts that do not require high memory or parallel processing.
description: Vultr vhf-1c-1gb is an entry-level virtual machine in the High Frequency Compute family, featuring 1 shared Intel Skylake vCPU core clocked at 2.0 GHz, 1.0 GB of RAM, and 32 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the bottom 10% due to the single-core, shared-resource design. This server offers high qualitative cost efficiency for low-resource workloads, trading off raw performance for budget-friendly hosting. It is suitable for basic web serving, lightweight utility scripts, and development testing environments.
